title: Memcached Connection Count Approaching Limit
app: Custom scripted input that appends `maxconns` from `memcached -h` / config to each stats event
spl:
index=infrastructure sourcetype="memcached:stats"
| eval cur=tonumber(curr_connections)
| eval maxc=tonumber(maxconns)
| eval pct=if(maxc>0, round(100*cur/maxc,1), null())
| where pct > 85 OR cur > 10000
description: Memcached refuses new connections when the limit is hit, which looks like random application failures. Tracking `curr_connections` against `maxconns` gives time to recycle pools or scale out.
value: Avoids incident bridges caused by connection storms during deploys or mis-sized pools.
implementation: Add `maxconns` from instance config to each poll in your collector. Default memcached limit varies by build—never assume 1024. Alert at 85% sustained 10 minutes. Pair with app-side pool metrics.
